paper_title: MOFs-derived C-In2O3\/g-C3N4 heterojunction for enhanced photoreduction CO2

catalyst.source: Then C-In 2 O 3 and g-C 3 N 4 are hydrothermal to obtain C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 heterojunction.
catalyst.context: Converting carbon dioxide into valuable chemicals is seen as a promising strategy for slowing global warming while enriching energy supplies.Photocatalysts derived from Metal-Organic Frameworks (MOFs) can not only overcome the problem of poor stability of MOFs, but also inherit the advantages of MOFs to some extent.Herein, carbon doped In 2 O 3 (C-In 2 O 3 ) hollow tubular is synthesized by calcination of MIL-68(In) as sacrificial template.Then C-In 2 O 3 and g-C 3 N 4 are hydrothermal to obtain C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 heterojunction.The unique hollow tubular structure, carbon doping and type-II heterojunction of C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 expand the light absorption capacity and promote the separation of photogenerated electron-hole pairs, thus significantly improve the activity of photocatalytic reduction of CO 2 .Among them, C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -5 shows the highest CO 2 reduction activity towards CO (153.42 molgh) and CH 4 (110.31molgh).

catalyst.source: Typically, the sealed stainless reactor involved 20 mg photocatalyst, 5 mL of triethanolamine (TEOA) and 95 mL of deionized water under UV light for 4 h.
catalyst.context: Terephthalic acid (C 8 H 6 O 4 ), urea (CO(NH 2 ) 2 ), N, N-dimethylformamide (C 3 H 7 NO), ethanol (C 2 H 5 OH) and nitric acid were all purchased from Sinopharm Chemical Reagent Co., Ltd.Indium Nitrate Hydrate (In (NO 3 ) 3 H 2 O) supplied by Aladdin Chemical Co., Ltd.All chemicals are analytical grade and no further purification is required when used. The protocol for the preparation of MIL-68(In) is adapted from a previous report with slight modifications 38,39.Typically, 468 mg of Indium nitrate hydrate and 180 mg terephthalic acid were dissolved in 40 mL of DMF.Next, the solution was transferred to a round-bottomed flask for vigorous stirring for 15 min, followed by a 120 C oil bath for 30 min.After natural cooling down, the precursors were centrifugally separated and washed three times with water and ethanol.The white precipitate was vacuum dried at 80 C for 12 h. The as-obtained MIL-68(In) were first annealed in air at 120 C for 2 h with a heating rate of 5 Cmin -1 , and then further annealed at 500 C for 2 h with the same heating rate 40. The preparation method of g-C 3 N 4 was modified based on previous literature 41.Briefly, after grinding the urea for 30 min, it is calcined in a muffle oven at 550 C for 4 h at a heating rate of 5 Cmin.After natural cooling, the products of the first calcination were placed in a mixed solution of 200 mL deionized water and 0.5 mL nitric acid in a water bath at 80 C for 10 h.G-C 3 N 4 was then centrifuged out, washed with deionized water for 5 times, and dried in vacuum at 80 C. Finally, the treated g-C 3 N 4 is calcined at 550 C at the same heating rate for 2 h. In short, a certain amount of g-C 3 N 4 was dispersed in 50 mL deionized water, treated with ultrasound for 1 h, and then 50 mg C-In 2 O 3 was added for stirring for 1 h.Finally, suspension was transferred into a mL Teflon-lined autoclave and put in a preheated oven at 100 C for 8 h. The photocatalytic CO 2 conversion reactions were performed in a closed photochemical reactor.Typically, the sealed stainless reactor involved 20 mg photocatalyst, 5 mL of triethanolamine (TEOA) and 95 mL of deionized water under UV light for 4 h.Then, before the reaction starts, high purity carbon dioxide (99.999) should be fed into the reactor for at least 20 min, after which the stainless reactor exhaust valve would be closed to achieve 0.4 MPa of gas pressure.Finally, the magnetic agitator is used to stir the reaction system during the reaction.In the process of photocatalytic reaction, gas product of the CO 2 photo reduction was detected by gas chromatograph with FID detector every 1 h.

catalyst.source: Therefore, the type-II heterojunction formed by C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -5 greatly improves the photocatalytic performance, which the CO and CH 4 yields of C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -5 are 6.15 and 9.5 times of than that of MIL-68(In).
catalyst.context: In summary, we obtained tubular C-In 2 O 3 through MIL-68(In) calcination, which greatly improved its light capture ability and electron transport ability.Then, g-C 3 N 4 is supported on the surface of C-In 2 O 3 by hydrothermal, so as to change the electron transport mode and effectively inhibit the recombination of electron hole pairs.At the same time, the residual C element can also be used as electron transport medium to accelerate the transmission of photogenerated carriers.Therefore, the type-II heterojunction formed by C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -5 greatly improves the photocatalytic performance, which the CO and CH 4 yields of C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -5 are 6.15 and 9.5 times of than that of MIL-68(In). Scheme 1. Schematic diagram of synthesis process of C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 . Fig. 1.(a) The XRD patterns of all the prepared samples; (b) Local amplification of XRD spectra of C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -10, C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -7, C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -5, C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -3, C-In 2 O 3 and In 2 O 3 . The resulting product was centrifuged and successively washed with H 2 O and ethanol, then dried at 120 C under vacuum for 12 h.The C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -3, C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -5, C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -7 and C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -10 were synthesized in different proportions of mass.For example, in the sample C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -3, the mass ratio of g-C 3 N 4 and C-In 2 O was 3:100 (Scheme 1). Fig. 2. The XPS of g-C 3 N 4 , C-In 2 O 3 and C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -5: (a) survey, (b) C 1s, (c) N 1s, (d) O 1s and (e) In 3d. , the XPS survey spectra of C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -5 contains elements of C-In 2 O 3 and g-C 3 N 4 .In Fig. 2b, we can see that the signal peaks of C 1s of C-In 2 O 3 appear at 288.70, 286.20 and 284.88 eV, corresponding to C -O--C bond, sp 2 -bonded carbon (N -CN--NH 2 ) and graphite carbon (C -C) respectively.Notably, a new peak appears at 288.30 eV in C- Fig. 2c, the N element of g-C 3 N 4 shows three signal peaks at 401.05, 399.94 and 395.50 eV, which can be attributed to the surface C -N -H groups, N-(C) 3 and the sp 2 -hybridized C-N--C 49.After combination with C-In 2 O 3 , the peaks move to the direction of decreasing binding energy, changing to 401.40, 399.75 and 398.40 eV, respectively.In Fig. 2d, for C-In 2 O 3 , the three peaks appear at 531.74, 530.68 and 529.6 eV could be indexed as adsorbed oxygen, oxygen vacancy and lattice oxygen respectively.However, the peak positions of O element in C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -5 appear at 531.78, 530.80 and 529.99 eV, the blue shift occurred 50.Similarly (Fig. 2e), the peak position of In element in C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -5 was also more positive than that of C-In 2 O 3 , which changed from 451.81 and 444.27 eV in C-In 2 O 3 to 452.22 and Fig. 3. SEM images of MIL-68(In) (a), C-In 2 O 3 (b), C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -5 (c) and TEM of C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -5 (d). Fig. 4. (a) UV-Vis Diffuse reflection spectra (DRS); (b) the band gap spectra of C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -5, g-C 3 N 4 and C-In 2 O 3 photocatalysts; Mott-Schottky plots of g-C 3 N 4 (c) and C-In2O3 (d); The valence band spectra of g-C 3 N 4 (e) and C-In 2 O 3 (f). Fig. 5. (a) The N 2 adsorptiondesorption isotherms curves, (b) pore diameter distribution of C-In 2 O 3 , C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -5 and MIL-68(In) photocatalysts. Fig. 6.Time-yield plots of CO (a) and CH 4 (b) for different photocatalysts; (c) Production rate of CO; (d) Production rate of CH 4 . H 2 OCO);4) CO desorption releases CO (CO CO); 5) The CO participates in the next reaction step for CH 4 generation.In order to have a more intuitive understanding of the operation principle of C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -5 heterostructure, combined with UV-Vis diffuse reflectance spectroscopy (DRS), Mott-Schottky plots and valence band (VB) spectra of XPS, the mechanism diagram of CO reduction under UV lamp irradiation was made (Fig. 9).As shown in the diagram, this is a conventional type-II heterojunction.The valence band and conduction band of g-C 3 N 4 and C-In 2 O 3 are 1.60 eV and -1.20 eV, 1.69 eV and -0.81 eV, respectively, which meet the conditions of reducing CO 2 into CO and CH 4 (E CO2CO -0.53 V vs NHE at pH and E CO2CH4 -0.24 V vs NHE at pH 7) 77.When g-C 3 N 4 and C-In 2 O 3 are excited by ultraviolet light, the generated electrons and Fig. 7. (a) The transient photocurrent; (b) EIS plot of as-synthesized samples; the steady-state PL (c) and time-resolved PL (d) of C-In 2 O 3 , g-C 3 N 4 and C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -5. Fig. 8.In situ FTIR spectra of 20-CdSNi-MOF C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -5. Investigation, Writingoriginal draft, Methodology.Xiaoxue Zhao: Investigation, Data curation, Formal analysis.Haopeng Jiang: Validation.Songtao Chen: Writingreview editing.Pengwei Huo: Supervision, Conceptualization, Writing -review editing, Funding acquisition. Fig. 9. Schematic diagram of electron transfer process and photocatalytic mechanism of photocatalytic reduction of CO 2 . , 35.1 , 51.8 and 62.1 correspond to (211), ( 2 by C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -5: 1) CO 2 is adsorbed on the surface of the catalyst (CO 2 CO 2 ); 2) The adsorbed and activated CO 2 reacts with H and e -to obtain an important intermediate product COOH (CO 2 H e -COOH); 3) COOH is converted to H 2 O and CO via a proton-electron transfer reduction process (COOHH e - This work was supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China (22078131). The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper. Supplementary data associated with this article can be found in the online version at doi:10.1016j.jece.2021.106469.

The resulting product was centrifuged and successively washed with H 2 O and ethanol, then dried at 120 C under vacuum for 12 h.The C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -3, C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -5, C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -7 and C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -10 were synthesized in different proportions of mass.For example, in the sample C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -3, the mass ratio of g-C 3 N 4 and C-In 2 O was 3:100 (Scheme 1). The XPS of g-C 3 N 4 , C-In 2 O 3 and C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -5: (a) survey, (b) C 1s, (c) N 1s, (d) O 1s and (e) In 3d. , the XPS survey spectra of C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -5 contains elements of C-In 2 O 3 and g-C 3 N 4 .In Fig. 2b, we can see that the signal peaks of C 1s of C-In 2 O 3 appear at 288.70, 286.20 and 284.88 eV, corresponding to C -O--C bond, sp 2 -bonded carbon (N -CN--NH 2 ) and graphite carbon (C -C) respectively.Notably, a new peak appears at 288.30 eV in C- Fig. 2c, the N element of g-C 3 N 4 shows three signal peaks at 401.05, 399.94 and 395.50 eV, which can be attributed to the surface C -N -H groups, N-(C) 3 and the sp 2 -hybridized C-N--C 49.After combination with C-In 2 O 3 , the peaks move to the direction of decreasing binding energy, changing to 401.40, 399.75 and 398.40 eV, respectively.In Fig. 2d, for C-In 2 O 3 , the three peaks appear at 531.74, 530.68 and 529.6 eV could be indexed as adsorbed oxygen, oxygen vacancy and lattice oxygen respectively.However, the peak positions of O element in C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -5 appear at 531.78, 530.80 and 529.99 eV, the blue shift occurred 50.Similarly (Fig. 2e), the peak position of In element in C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -5 was also more positive than that of C-In 2 O 3 , which changed from 451.81 and 444.27 eV in C-In 2 O 3 to 452.22 and Fig. 3. H 2 OCO);4) CO desorption releases CO (CO CO); 5) The CO participates in the next reaction step for CH 4 generation.In order to have a more intuitive understanding of the operation principle of C-In 2 O 3 g-C 3 N 4 -5 heterostructure, combined with UV-Vis diffuse reflectance spectroscopy (DRS), Mott-Schottky plots and valence band (VB) spectra of XPS, the mechanism diagram of CO reduction under UV lamp irradiation was made (Fig. 9).As shown in the diagram, this is a conventional type-II heterojunction.The valence band and conduction band of g-C 3 N 4 and C-In 2 O 3 are 1.60 eV and -1.20 eV, 1.69 eV and -0.81 eV, respectively, which meet the conditions of reducing CO 2 into CO and CH 4 (E CO2CO -0.53 V vs NHE at pH and E CO2CH4 -0.24 V vs NHE at pH 7) 77.When g-C 3 N 4 and C-In 2 O 3 are excited by ultraviolet light, the generated electrons and Fig. 7. reaction medium.source: Typically, the sealed stainless reactor involved 20 mg photocatalyst, 5 mL of triethanolamine (TEOA) and 95 mL of deionized water under UV light for 4 h.
reaction medium.context: Terephthalic acid (C 8 H 6 O 4 ), urea (CO(NH 2 ) 2 ), N, N-dimethylformamide (C 3 H 7 NO), ethanol (C 2 H 5 OH) and nitric acid were all purchased from Sinopharm Chemical Reagent Co., Ltd.Indium Nitrate Hydrate (In (NO 3 ) 3 H 2 O) supplied by Aladdin Chemical Co., Ltd.All chemicals are analytical grade and no further purification is required when used. The protocol for the preparation of MIL-68(In) is adapted from a previous report with slight modifications 38,39.Typically, 468 mg of Indium nitrate hydrate and 180 mg terephthalic acid were dissolved in 40 mL of DMF.Next, the solution was transferred to a round-bottomed flask for vigorous stirring for 15 min, followed by a 120 C oil bath for 30 min.After natural cooling down, the precursors were centrifugally separated and washed three times with water and ethanol.The white precipitate was vacuum dried at 80 C for 12 h. The as-obtained MIL-68(In) were first annealed in air at 120 C for 2 h with a heating rate of 5 Cmin -1 , and then further annealed at 500 C for 2 h with the same heating rate 40. The preparation method of g-C 3 N 4 was modified based on previous literature 41.Briefly, after grinding the urea for 30 min, it is calcined in a muffle oven at 550 C for 4 h at a heating rate of 5 Cmin.After natural cooling, the products of the first calcination were placed in a mixed solution of 200 mL deionized water and 0.5 mL nitric acid in a water bath at 80 C for 10 h.G-C 3 N 4 was then centrifuged out, washed with deionized water for 5 times, and dried in vacuum at 80 C. Finally, the treated g-C 3 N 4 is calcined at 550 C at the same heating rate for 2 h. In short, a certain amount of g-C 3 N 4 was dispersed in 50 mL deionized water, treated with ultrasound for 1 h, and then 50 mg C-In 2 O 3 was added for stirring for 1 h.Finally, suspension was transferred into a mL Teflon-lined autoclave and put in a preheated oven at 100 C for 8 h. The photocatalytic CO 2 conversion reactions were performed in a closed photochemical reactor.Typically, the sealed stainless reactor involved 20 mg photocatalyst, 5 mL of triethanolamine (TEOA) and 95 mL of deionized water under UV light for 4 h.Then, before the reaction starts, high purity carbon dioxide (99.999) should be fed into the reactor for at least 20 min, after which the stainless reactor exhaust valve would be closed to achieve 0.4 MPa of gas pressure.Finally, the magnetic agitator is used to stir the reaction system during the reaction.In the process of photocatalytic reaction, gas product of the CO 2 photo reduction was detected by gas chromatograph with FID detector every 1 h.

operation mode.source: Then, before the reaction starts, high purity carbon dioxide (99.999) should be fed into the reactor for at least 20 min, after which the stainless reactor exhaust valve would be closed to achieve 0.4 MPa of gas pressure.
operation mode.context: Terephthalic acid (C 8 H 6 O 4 ), urea (CO(NH 2 ) 2 ), N, N-dimethylformamide (C 3 H 7 NO), ethanol (C 2 H 5 OH) and nitric acid were all purchased from Sinopharm Chemical Reagent Co., Ltd.Indium Nitrate Hydrate (In (NO 3 ) 3 H 2 O) supplied by Aladdin Chemical Co., Ltd.All chemicals are analytical grade and no further purification is required when used. The protocol for the preparation of MIL-68(In) is adapted from a previous report with slight modifications 38,39.Typically, 468 mg of Indium nitrate hydrate and 180 mg terephthalic acid were dissolved in 40 mL of DMF.Next, the solution was transferred to a round-bottomed flask for vigorous stirring for 15 min, followed by a 120 C oil bath for 30 min.After natural cooling down, the precursors were centrifugally separated and washed three times with water and ethanol.The white precipitate was vacuum dried at 80 C for 12 h. The as-obtained MIL-68(In) were first annealed in air at 120 C for 2 h with a heating rate of 5 Cmin -1 , and then further annealed at 500 C for 2 h with the same heating rate 40. The preparation method of g-C 3 N 4 was modified based on previous literature 41.Briefly, after grinding the urea for 30 min, it is calcined in a muffle oven at 550 C for 4 h at a heating rate of 5 Cmin.After natural cooling, the products of the first calcination were placed in a mixed solution of 200 mL deionized water and 0.5 mL nitric acid in a water bath at 80 C for 10 h.G-C 3 N 4 was then centrifuged out, washed with deionized water for 5 times, and dried in vacuum at 80 C. Finally, the treated g-C 3 N 4 is calcined at 550 C at the same heating rate for 2 h. In short, a certain amount of g-C 3 N 4 was dispersed in 50 mL deionized water, treated with ultrasound for 1 h, and then 50 mg C-In 2 O 3 was added for stirring for 1 h.Finally, suspension was transferred into a mL Teflon-lined autoclave and put in a preheated oven at 100 C for 8 h. The photocatalytic CO 2 conversion reactions were performed in a closed photochemical reactor.Typically, the sealed stainless reactor involved 20 mg photocatalyst, 5 mL of triethanolamine (TEOA) and 95 mL of deionized water under UV light for 4 h.Then, before the reaction starts, high purity carbon dioxide (99.999) should be fed into the reactor for at least 20 min, after which the stainless reactor exhaust valve would be closed to achieve 0.4 MPa of gas pressure.Finally, the magnetic agitator is used to stir the reaction system during the reaction.In the process of photocatalytic reaction, gas product of the CO 2 photo reduction was detected by gas chromatograph with FID detector every 1 h.
